name: Climb to the ports of Muela de San Juan and Puerto de Noguera — circular from Checa
name_original: Subida a los puertos de Muela de San Juan y Puerto de Noguera — circular desde Checa
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Checa are set within the Alto Tajo Natural Park, offering diverse terrain at elevations between 1,360 and 1,480 meters. The region features picturesque valleys flanked by hills, dense pine forests, and spectacular ravines carved by abundant streams. Cyclists can explore quiet roads through open landscapes, encountering unique geological formations and varied botanical interests. Best road cycling routes around Checa The most popular road cycling route is Serranía de Cuenca and Alto Tajo Loop, a 79.1 miles (127.3 km) trail that takes 6 hours 35 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses the natural park's varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Climb to the ports of Muela de San Juan and Puerto de Noguera — circular from Checa, a difficult 57.4 miles (92.3 km) path. This circular route from Checa features significant elevation gains through mountainous terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Birth of the Tajo River - circular through the Montes Universales, a 88.1 miles (141.9 km) trail leading through the Montes Universales, often completed in about 7 hours 30 minutes. Road cycling around Checa is defined by dense pine forests, deep ravines, and high-altitude valleys within the Alto Tajo Natural Park. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Checa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Checa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Checa?
A: There are over 10 road cycling routes around Checa, offering diverse terrain within the Alto Tajo Natural Park. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level for road cycling routes in Checa?
A: Road cycling in Checa is generally challenging due to the region's high elevation, ranging from 1,360 to 1,480 meters above sea level. Most routes are classified as difficult, with 11 out of 12 routes falling into this category. There is one moderate route available for those seeking a slightly less demanding ride.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Checa?
A: Yes, Checa offers excellent circular road cycling options. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017674219/climb-to-the-ports-of-muela-de-san-juan-and-puerto-de-noguera-circular-from-checa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to the ports of Muela de San Juan and Puerto de Noguera — circular from Checa&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 57.4-mile (92.3 km) path featuring significant elevation gains through mountainous terrain.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while road cycling in Checa?
A: Road cycling routes around Checa traverse stunning natural landscapes within the Alto Tajo Natural Park. You'll encounter picturesque valleys flanked by hills, dense pine forests like the Dehesa de La Espineda, and spectacular ravines carved by abundant streams. The region also features unique geological formations, such as the red sandstone landscapes near Chequilla, and varied botanical interests.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints or natural attractions along the road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in natural beauty. You can find unique rock formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2094395" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The towers of Chequilla&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4740317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Cuervo River&lt;/a&gt;. The area also boasts impressive canyons such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5181829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peralejos de las Truchas Canyon&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5474397" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estrecho del Infierno&lt;/a&gt;, offering breathtaking views.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Checa?
A: Given Checa's elevation (1,360-1,480 meters), the warmer months, typically from late spring through early autumn, offer the most favorable weather conditions for road cycling. During this period, you can enjoy open landscapes, quiet roads, and gentle hills without the challenges of colder temperatures or potential snow at higher altitudes.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in the Checa area?
A: Yes, Checa is an excellent base for long-distance road cycling. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963701107/birth-of-the-tajo-river-circular-through-the-montes-universales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birth of the Tajo River - circular through the Montes Universales&lt;/a&gt; span 88.1 miles (141.9 km) and can take around 7 hours 30 minutes. Another significant option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018223931/serrania-de-cuenca-and-alto-tajo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serranía de Cuenca and Alto Tajo Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 79.1-mile (127.3 km) trail.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Checa?
A: The komoot community highly rates road cycling in Checa, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the region's stunning natural landscapes, the quiet roads with minimal traffic, and the varied terrain that provides a rewarding challenge. The integration within the Alto Tajo Natural Park is also a significant draw.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical points of interest near the cycling routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, Checa and its surroundings offer cultural insights. You can explore traditional Molinés houses from the 18th century in Checa itself, or visit the 17th-century Church of San Juan Bautista. For a glimpse into the past, archaeological sites like the Celtiberian settlement of Castil de Griegos are also nearby.

Q: What are some of the key features of the Alto Tajo Natural Park that make it suitable for road cycling?
A: The Alto Tajo Natural Park, where Checa is located, is renowned for its diverse habitats and invaluable natural landscapes. For road cycling, it offers a network of paths and forest tracks, including sections of the Camino Natural del Alto Tajo. The park's varied terrain, from dense pine forests to deep ravines and high-altitude valleys, provides both scenic beauty and challenging rides, with minimal traffic on its open roads.

Q: Are there any towns or settlements worth visiting while cycling in the area?
A: Yes, several charming settlements are worth exploring. The village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5167073" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Checa&lt;/a&gt; itself is a great starting point. Nearby, you can visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2530405" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orihuela del Tremedal&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5376107" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Griegos&lt;/a&gt;, which offer a glimpse into traditional Spanish village life and architecture.
tips: Nordic ski resort located at the top of the Santa Bárbera mountain, located more than 1800 meters high. From this point you can see a panoramic view of the town of Griegos.

Villar del Cobo is a beautiful town in the interior of Teruel that is located at the gates of the renowned Montes Universales and is part of the tourist trail that connects Guadalaviar with Albarracín. In the town you will find all the necessary services to resupply yourself or just make a stop on your way and enjoy this quiet place.

The Puerto de Noguera is an ascent that leads from Noguera d'Albarracín in the direction of the center of the Serra of the same name. Its maximum altitude is 1,695 meters and it is surrounded by typical pine trees of the area.
Orihuela del Tremedal has its roots in medieval times. It is believed that the town was founded in the 12th century, during the Reconquista, when Christians recovered territories from the Muslims.
